arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، قَالَ: لَا يُجْزِي فِي الصَّدَقَةِ ذَاتُ عَوَارٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mūsā ibn ‘Ubaydah → Sulaymān ibn Yasār
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Mūsā ibn ‘Ubaydah, who said: I heard Sulaymān ibn Yasār say: “A defective animal does not suffice for charity.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا كَثِيرُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مَيْمُونٍ، قَالَ: لَا يُؤْخَذُ فِي الصَّدَقَةِ الْعَجْفَاءُ، وَلَا الْعَوْرَاءُ، وَلَا الْجَرْبَاءُ، وَلَا الْعَرْجَاءُ الَّتِي لَا تَتْبَعُ الْغَنَمَ

english.narrator: Kathīr ibn Hishām → Hishām → Ja‘far ibn Maymūn
english.text: Kathīr ibn Hishām narrated to us, from Hishām, from Ja‘far ibn Maymūn, who said: “One should not take the emaciated, nor the one-eyed, nor the scabby, nor the lame one that cannot follow the flock, as Zakat.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنْ عُمَارَةَ، أَنَّ أَبَاهُ يَحْيَى بْنَ عُمَارَةَ، أَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّ أَبَا سَعِيدٍ كَانَ يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَيْسَ فِيمَا دُونَ خَمْسَةِ أَوْسُقٍ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → ‘Amr ibn Yaḥyā → ‘Umārah → Yaḥyā ibn ‘Umārah → Abū Sa‘īd
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from ‘Amr ibn Yaḥyā, from ‘Umārah, that his father Yaḥyā ibn ‘Umārah informed him that Abū Sa‘īd used to say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no Zakat on less than five Wasqs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ أُمَيَّةَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ حِبَّانَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ عُمَارَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَيْسَ فِي أَقَلَّ مِنْ خَمْسَةِ أَوْسُقٍ حَبٍّ، وَلَا تَمْرٍ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Ismā‘īl ibn Umayyah → Muḥammad ibn Yaḥyā ibn Ḥibbān → Yaḥyā ibn ‘Umārah → Abū Sa‘īd
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Ismā‘īl ibn Umayyah, from Muḥammad ibn Yaḥyā ibn Ḥibbān, from Yaḥyā ibn ‘Umārah, from Abū Sa‘īd, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no Zakat on less than five Wasqs of grain or dates.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَيْسَ مِمَّا دُونَ خَمْسَةِ أَوْسَاقٍ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → ‘Amr ibn Yaḥyā → His Father → Abū Sa‘īd
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from ‘Amr ibn Yaḥyā, from his father, from Abū Sa‘īd, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no Zakat on less than five Wasqs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، وَعَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِيمَا دُونَ خَمْسَةِ، أَوْسَاقٍ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Khālid → Ash‘ath → Ayyūb → Abū Qilābah, Abū al-Zubayr → Jābir
english.text: Abū Khālid narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Ayyūb, from Abū Qilābah, and from Abū al-Zubayr, from Jābir, who said: “There is no Zakat on less than five Wasqs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ جَعْفَرٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: إِذَا بَلَغَ الطَّعَامُ خَمْسَةَ أَوْسُقٍ فَفِيهِ الصَّدَقَةُ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → Ja‘far → His Father
english.text: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Ja‘far, from his father, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “If the food (produce) reaches five Wasqs, then Zakat is due on it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِيمَا دُونَ خَمْسَةِ أَوْسَاقٍ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm → Muḥammad ibn Sālim → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Sālim, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “There is no Zakat on less than five Wasqs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، وَعَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَا تَجِبُ الصَّدَقَةُ حَتَّى تَبْلُغَ ثَلَاثَمِائَةِ صَاعٍ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm, Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, and from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “Zakat is not obligatory until it reaches three hundred Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي أَقَلَّ مِنْ خَمْسَةِ أَوْسُقٍ شَيْءٌ

english.narrator: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Abd al-Karīm → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb → His Father → His Grandfather
english.text: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Abd al-Karīm, from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: “There is nothing [Zakat] due on less than five Wasqs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ مُرَّةَ، عَنْ أَبِي الْبَخْتَرِيِّ، عَنْ أَبِي سَعِيدٍ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Sharīk → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Amr ibn Murrah → Abū al-Bakhtarī → Abū Sa‘īd
english.text: Sharīk narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Amr ibn Murrah, from Abū al-Bakhtarī, from Abū Sa‘īd, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ شَرِيكٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sharīk → Layth → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sharīk, from Layth, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Khālid → Abū Qilābah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Khālid, from Abū Qilābah, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَمُغِيرَةُ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughīrah → Al-Ḥasan, Mughīrah, Ibrāhīm
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Al-Ḥasan, and Mughīrah from Ibrāhīm, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، وَمُحَمَّدٍ، قَالَا: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan, Muḥammad
english.text: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Ḥasan and Muḥammad, who both said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا خَالِدٌ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ أَبِي قِلَابَةَ، وَعَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ خَالِدٍ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Khālid al-Aḥmar → Ash‘ath → Ayyūb → Abū Qilābah, Abū al-Zubayr → Khālid
english.text: Khālid al-Aḥmar narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Ayyūb, from Abū Qilābah, and from Abū al-Zubayr, from Khālid, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm → Muḥammad ibn Sālim → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Sālim, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا رَوَّادُ بْنُ الْجَرَّاحِ، عَنِ الْأَوْزَاعِيِّ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Rawwād ibn al-Jarrāḥ → Al-Awzā‘ī → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: Rawwād ibn al-Jarrāḥ narrated to us, from Al-Awzā‘ī, from Al-Zuhrī, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَصْحَابُنَا، عَنِ ابْنِ الْمُبَارَكِ، عَنْ يَعْقُوبَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ الْمُسَيِّبِ قَالَ: الْوَسْقُ سِتُّونَ صَاعًا

english.narrator: Our Companions → Ibn al-Mubārak → Ya‘qūb → ‘Aṭā’ → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Our companions narrated to us, from Ibn al-Mubārak, from Ya‘qūb, from ‘Aṭā’, from Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib, who said: “The Wasq is sixty Sā‘.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: الْعُشْرُ فِي التَّمْرِ، وَالزَّبِيبِ، وَالْحِنْطَةِ، وَالشَّعِيرِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Alī ibn Hāshim → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Abd al-Karīm → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb → His Father → His Grandfather
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim narrated to us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Abd al-Karīm, from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: “The tithe (Zakat) is on dates, raisins, wheat, and barley.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ عُثْمَانَ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ طَلْحَةَ، أَنَّ مُعَاذًا، لَمَّا قَدِمَ الْيَمَنَ لَمْ يَأْخُذِ الزَّكَاةَ، إِلَّا مِنَ الْحِنْطَةِ، وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالزَّبِيبِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → ‘Amr ibn ‘Uthmān → Mūsā ibn Ṭalḥah → Mu‘ādh
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from ‘Amr ibn ‘Uthmān, from Mūsā ibn Ṭalḥah, that when Mu‘ādh arrived in Yemen, “He did not take Zakat except from wheat, barley, dates, and raisins.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ يَحْيَى، عَنْ أَبِي بُرْدَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي مُوسَى الْأَشْعَرِيِّ، أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَأْخُذْهَا إِلَّا مِنَ الْحِنْطَةِ، وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالزَّبِيبِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ṭalḥah ibn Yaḥyā → Abū Burdah → Abū Mūsā al-Ash‘arī
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ṭalḥah ibn Yaḥyā, from Abū Burdah, from Abū Mūsā al-Ash‘arī, that he “did not take it (Zakat) except from wheat, barley, dates, and raisins.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: الصَّدَقَةُ عَنْ أَرْبَعٍ مِنَ الْبُرِّ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ بُرٌّ فَتَمْرٌ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ تَمْرٌ فَزَبِيبٌ، فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ زَبِيبٌ فَشَعِيرٌ

english.narrator: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → His Father → Abū Isḥāq → Al-Ḥārith → ‘Alī
english.text: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from his father, from Abū Isḥāq, from Al-Ḥārith, from ‘Alī, who said: “Charity [Zakat] is from four things: wheat; if there is no wheat, then dates; if there are no dates, then raisins; if there are no raisins, then barley.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ بْنِ يَحْيَى، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَبْدَ الْحَمِيدِ، وَمُوسَى بْنَ طَلْحَةَ عَنْهَا، فَقَالَا: إِنَّمَا الصَّدَقَةُ فِي الْحِنْطَةِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالزَّبِيبِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ṭalḥah ibn Yaḥyā → ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd, Mūsā ibn Ṭalḥah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ṭalḥah ibn Yaḥyā, who said: I asked ‘Abd al-Ḥamīd and Mūsā ibn Ṭalḥah about it, and they said: “Charity [Zakat] is only on wheat, dates, and raisins.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ لِي عَطَاءٌ: لَا صَدَقَةَ إِلَّا فِي نَخْلٍ، أَوْ عِنَبٍ، أَوْ حَبٍّ. وَقَالَ لِي ذَلِكَ عَمْرُو بْنُ دِينَارٍ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’, ‘Amr ibn Dīnār
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: ‘Aṭā’ said to me: “There is no Zakat except on palm trees (dates), grapes, or grain.” ‘Amr ibn Dīnār also said that to me.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: الزَّكَاةُ فِي الْبُرِّ، وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالزَّبِيبِ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “Zakat is on wheat, barley, dates, and raisins.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا مَعْمَرُ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ الرَّقِّيُّ، عَنْ خُصَيْفٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، قَالَ: فِيمَا أَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ فِيمَا قَلَّ مِنْهُ، أَوْ كَثُرَ الْعُشْرُ، أَوْ نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ma‘mar ibn Sulaymān al-Raqqī → Khuṣayf → Mujāhid
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ma‘mar ibn Sulaymān al-Raqqī narrated to us, from Khuṣayf, from Mujāhid, who said: “For whatever the earth produces, whether little or much, there is a tithe (Ushr) or half a tithe.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: فِي كُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ الْعُشْرُ، أَوْ نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Ḥammād
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Ḥammād, who said: “In everything the earth produces, there is a tithe (Ushr) or half a tithe.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي حَنِيفَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: فِي كُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ زَكَاةٌ حَتَّى فِي عَشْرِ دَسْتَجَاتِ بَقْلٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abū Ḥanīfah → Ḥammād → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Abū Ḥanīfah, from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “There is Zakat on everything the earth produces, even on ten bunches of vegetables.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُوَقِّتُ فِي الثَّمَرِ شَيْئًا، وَقَالَ: الْعُشْرُ، وَنِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ.

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Ma‘mar → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhrī, that he used to not set a specific threshold for fruit, and he said: “The tenth (Ushr) and half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، مِثْلَهُ.

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Ma‘mar → A Man → Mujāhid
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Ma‘mar, from a man, from Mujāhid, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، قَالَ: كَتَبَ بِذَلِكَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ إِلَى أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Ma‘mar
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Ma‘mar, who said: ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz wrote that to the people of Yemen.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: فِي كُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَخْرَجَتِ الْأَرْضُ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “In everything that the earth produces, there is Zakat.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو مُعَاوِيَةَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: فِي الْخَضْرَاوَاتِ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Mu‘āwiyah → Layth → Mujāhid → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Abū Mu‘āwiyah told us, from Layth, from Mujāhid, from Ibn ‘Umar, who said: “There is Zakat in green vegetables.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ قَيْسٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ ضَمْرَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخُضَرِ شَيْءٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Qays → Abū Isḥāq → ‘Āṣim ibn Ḍamrah → ‘Alī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Qays, from Abū Isḥāq, from ‘Āṣim ibn Ḍamrah, from ‘Alī, who said: “There is nothing (no Zakat) in green vegetables.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ مُجَالِدٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْبُقُولِ الْخِيَارِ، وَالْقِثَّاءِ، وَنَحْوِهِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Mujālid → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd told us, from Mujālid, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “There is no Sadaqah (Zakat) in herbs, cucumbers, snake cucumbers, and the like.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنِ الْأَجْلَحِ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي غَلَّةِ الصَّيْفِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → Al-Ajlaḥ → ‘Āmir
english.text: Ḥafṣ told us, from Al-Ajlaḥ, from ‘Āmir, who said: “There is no Sadaqah in the summer harvest.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ وَرْدَانَ، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخُضَرِ زَكَاةٌ، إِلَّا أَنْ يَصِيرَ مَالًا فَيَكُونَ فِيهِ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Ḥātim ibn Wardān → Burd → Makḥūl
english.text: Ḥātim ibn Wardān told us, from Burd, from Makḥūl, who said: “There is no Zakat in green vegetables, unless it becomes wealth (merchandise), then there is Zakat in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، قَالَ: سَمِعَتْ مُجَاهِدًا، وَإِبْرَاهِيمَ وَهُمَا جَالِسَانِ، يَقُولَانِ: لَيْسَ فِي الْبُقُولِ، وَلَا فِي التُّفَّاحِ، وَلَا فِي الْخُضَرِ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Mughīrah → Mujāhid + Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Mughīrah, who said: I heard Mujāhid and Ibrāhīm, while they were sitting, saying: “There is no Zakat in herbs, nor in apples, nor in green vegetables.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَضْرَاوَاتِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Sahl ibn Yūsuf → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Sahl ibn Yūsuf told us, from Shu‘bah, from Al-Ḥakam, who said: “There is no Sadaqah in green vegetables.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ حُصَيْنٍ، عَنْ مُطَرِّفٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَكَمَ، عَنِ الْفَصَافِصِ، وَالْأَقْطَانِ، وَالسَّمَاسِمِ، فَقَالَ: لَيْسَ فِيهَا شَيْءٌ قَالَ: الْحَكَمُ فِيمَا حَفِظْنَا عَنْ أَصْحَابِنَا، أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يَقُولُونَ: وَلَيْسَ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنْ هَذَا شَيْءٌ إِلَّا فِي الْحِنْطَةِ، وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالزَّبِيبِ

english.narrator: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → Ḥuṣayn → Muṭarrif → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān told us, from Ḥuṣayn, from Muṭarrif, who said: I asked Al-Ḥakam about clover (alfalfa), cotton, and sesame. He said: “There is nothing in them.” Al-Ḥakam said, regarding what we memorized from our companions, that they used to say: “There is nothing in any of this, except in wheat, barley, dates, and raisins.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ الْخُرَاسَانِيِّ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْفَاكِهَةِ عُشُورٌ الْجَوْزُ، وَاللَّوْزُ، وَالْبُقُولُ كُلُّهَا، وَالْخُضَرُ، وَلَكِنْ مَا بِيعَ مِنْهُ فَيَبْلُغُ مِائَتَيْ دِرْهَمٍ فَصَاعِدًا فَفِيهِ الزَّكَاةُ

english.narrator: Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Ayyāsh → ‘Aṭā’ al-Khurāsānī
english.text: Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Ayyāsh told us, from ‘Aṭā’ al-Khurāsānī, who said: “There are no tithes (Ushrs) in fruit: walnuts, almonds, all herbs, and green vegetables. However, whatever is sold from it and reaches two hundred dirhams and above, then there is Zakat in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عَطَاءٌ: لَيْسَ فِي الْبُقُولِ، وَالْقَصَبِ، وَالْخِرْبَزِ، وَالْقِثَّاءِ، وَالْكُرْسُفِ، وَالْفَوَاكِهِ، وَالْأُتْرُجِّ، وَالتُّفَّاحِ، وَالتِّينِ، وَالرُّمَّانِ، وَالْمِرْسَكِ، وَالْفَاكِهَةِ، يُعَدُّ كُلُّهَا مِمَّا فِيهِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: ‘Aṭā’ said: “There is nothing in herbs, reeds, melons, snake cucumbers, cotton, fruits, citrons, apples, figs, pomegranates, Mirsak, and fruit—all of it is (not) counted as that which has Sadaqah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الصَّمَدِ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سَلَمَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ بْنِ الشِّخِّيرِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْأَعْلَافِ، وَلَا فِي الْبُقُولِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Ṣamad → Ḥammād ibn Salamah → ‘Alī ibn al-Ḥakam → Abū al-‘Alā’ ibn al-Shikhkhīr
english.text: ‘Abd al-Ṣamad told us, from Ḥammād ibn Salamah, from ‘Alī ibn al-Ḥakam, from Abū al-‘Alā’ ibn al-Shikhkhīr, who said: “There is no Sadaqah in fodder, nor in herbs.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ فِي الزَّيْتُونِ، قَالَ: هُوَ مَكَالٌ فِيهِ الْعُشْرُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Mubārak → Ma‘mar → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: Abū Bakr told us: Ibn Mubārak told us, from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhrī regarding olives, he said: “It is measured, and there is a tenth (Ushr) in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مَهْدِيٍّ، عَنْ عِمْرَانَ الْقَطَّانِ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: فِي الزَّيْتُونِ الْعُشْرُ

english.narrator: Ibn Mahdī → ‘Imrān al-Qaṭṭān → Layth → Ṭāwūs → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn Mahdī told us, from ‘Imrān al-Qaṭṭān, from Layth, from Ṭāwūs, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: “In olives, there is the tenth (Ushr).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ رَجَاءِ بْنِ أَبِي سَلَمَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ يَزِيدَ بْنِ جَابِرٍ عَنْ الزَّيْتُونِ فَقَالَ: عَشَّرَهُ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ بِالشَّامِ.

english.narrator: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb → Rajā’ ibn Abī Salamah → Yazīd ibn Jābir
english.text: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb told us, from Rajā’ ibn Abī Salamah, who said: I asked Yazīd ibn Jābir about olives. He said: “‘Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb took the tenth from it in Al-Shām (Syria).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا زَيْدُ بْنُ حُبَابٍ، عَنْ رَجَاءٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ الْخُرَاسَانِيِّ، قَالَ: فِيهِ الْعُشْرُ

english.narrator: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb → Rajā’ → ‘Aṭā’ al-Khurāsānī
english.text: Zayd ibn Ḥubāb told us, from Rajā’, from ‘Aṭā’ al-Khurāsānī, who said: “In it (olives) is the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ مُوسَى، عَنْ أَبِي سَيَّارَةَ الْمُتُعِيِّ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ: إِنَّ لِي نَحْلًا قَالَ: أَمِنْهُ الْعُشْرُ قَالَ: نَعَمْ، قُلْتُ: يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ احْمِهَا لِي قَالَ: فَحَمَاهَا لِي

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sa‘īd ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz → Sulaymān ibn Mūsā → Abū Sayyārah al-Mutu‘ī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sa‘īd ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz, from Sulaymān ibn Mūsā, from Abū Sayyārah al-Mutu‘ī, who said: I said, “O Messenger of Allah, I have bees.” He said: “Do you pay the tenth (Ushr) from them?” I said, “Yes.” I said, “O Messenger of Allah, protect it (the valley) for me.” He said: “So he protected it for me.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبَّادُ بْنُ عَوَّامٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، أَنَّ أَمِيرَ الطَّائِفِ، كَتَبَ إِلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ الْخَطَّابِ، أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْعَسَلِ مَنَعُونَا مَا كَانُوا يُعْطُونَ مَنْ كَانَ قَبْلَنَا قَالَ: فَكَتَبَ إِلَيْهِ أَنْ أَعْطَوْكَ مَا كَانُوا يُعْطُونَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَاحْمِ لَهُمْ وَإِلَّا فَلَا تَحْمِهَا لَهُمْ قَالَ: وَزَعَمَ عَمْرُو بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا يُعْطُونَ مِنْ كُلِّ عَشْرِ قِرَبٍ قِرْبَةً

english.narrator: ‘Abbād ibn ‘Awwām → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb → The Governor of Aṭ-Ṭā’if → ‘Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb
english.text: ‘Abbād ibn ‘Awwām told us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb, that the governor of Aṭ-Ṭā’if wrote to ‘Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb that the honey producers withheld from us what they used to give to those before us. He said: So he wrote to him: “If they give you what they used to give the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, then protect (their valleys) for them; otherwise, do not protect them for them.” He said: And ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb claimed that they used to give one skin out of every ten skins.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ الْمُبَارَكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ الْخُرَاسَانِيِّ، عَنْ عُمَرَ، قَالَ: فِي الْعَسَلِ عُشْرٌ

english.narrator: Ibn al-Mubārak → ‘Aṭā’ al-Khurāsānī → ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn al-Mubārak told us, from ‘Aṭā’ al-Khurāsānī, from ‘Umar, who said: “In honey, there is a tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا صَفْوَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنْ مُنِيرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ أَبِي ذُبَابٍ، أَنَّهُ قَدِمَ عَلَى قَوْمِهِ فَقَالَ لَهُمْ: فِي الْعَسَلِ زَكَاةٌ فَإِنَّهُ لَا خَيْرَ فِي مَالٍ لَا يُزَكَّى قَالَ: قَالُوا: فَكَمْ تَرَى؟ قُلْتُ: الْعُشْرُ، فَأَخَذَ مِنْهُمُ الْعُشْرَ فَقَدِمَ بِهِ عَلَى عُمَرَ وَأَخْبَرَهُ بِمَا فِيهِ قَالَ: فَأَخَذَهُ عُمَرُ وَجَعَلَهُ فِي صَدَقَاتِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ

english.narrator: Ṣafwān ibn ‘Īsā → Al-Ḥārith ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → Munīr ibn ‘Abd Allāh → His Father → Sa‘d ibn Abī Dhubāb
english.text: Ṣafwān ibn ‘Īsā told us, from Al-Ḥārith ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān, from Munīr ibn ‘Abd Allāh, from his father, from Sa‘d ibn Abī Dhubāb, that he came to his people and said to them: “There is Zakat in honey, for there is no good in wealth that is not purified (by Zakat).” He said: They said: “How much do you see?” I said: “The tenth.” So he took the tenth from them and came with it to ‘Umar and informed him about it. He said: So ‘Umar took it and placed it in the Sadaqat of the Muslims.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي ذِئْبٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ: فِي الْعَسَلِ الْعُشْرُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ibn Abī Dhi’b → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ibn Abī Dhi’b, from Al-Zuhrī, who said: “In honey, there is the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَنْ قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْعَسَلِ زَكَاةٌ حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ مَيْسَرَةَ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، أَنَّ مُعَاذًا، لَمَّا أَتَى الْيَمَنَ، أَتَى الْعَسَلَ، وَأَوْقَاصَ الْغَنَمِ، فَقَالَ: لَمْ أُؤْمَرْ فِيهَا بِشَيْءٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Ibrāhīm ibn Maysarah → Ṭāwūs → Mu‘ādh
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Ibrāhīm ibn Maysarah, from Ṭāwūs, that when Mu‘ādh came to Yemen, honey and Awqāṣ (fractions between Zakat thresholds) of sheep were brought to him, and he said: “I have not been ordered anything regarding them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: بَعَثَنِي عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ عَلَى الْيَمَنِ فَأَرَدْتُ أَنْ آخُذَ مِنَ الْعَسَلِ الْعُشْرَ، قَالَ مُغِيرَةُ بْنُ حَكِيمٍ الصَّنْعَانِيُّ: لَيْسَ فِيهِ شَيْءٌ، فَكَتَبَتْ إِلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ فَقَالَ: صَدَقَ وَهُوَ عَدْلٌ رَضِيٌّ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → ‘Ubayd Allāh → Nāfi‘
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from ‘Ubayd Allāh, from Nāfi‘, who said: ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz sent me to Yemen, and I wanted to take the tenth from honey. Mughīrah ibn Ḥakīm al-Ṣan‘ānī said: “There is nothing in it.” So I wrote to ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz, and he said: “He spoke the truth, and he is a pleasing and just person.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلَنِي عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ عَنْ صَدَقَةِ الْعَسَلِ، فَقُلْتُ: أَخْبَرَنِي الْمُغِيرَةُ بْنُ حَكِيمٍ، أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ فِيهِ صَدَقَةٌ. فَقَالَ عُمَرُ: عَدْلٌ مُصَدَّقٌ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar → Nāfi‘
english.text: Abū Usāmah told us, from ‘Ubayd Allāh ibn ‘Umar, from Nāfi‘, who said: ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz asked me about the Sadaqah of honey. I said: Mughīrah ibn Ḥakīm informed me that there is no Sadaqah in it. ‘Umar said: “A just, truthful person.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَنْ قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْعَنْبَرِ زَكَاةٌ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ ابْنِ أُذَيْنَةَ، سَمِعَ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ الْعَنْبَرُ بِرِكَازٍ وَإِنَّمَا هُوَ شَيْءٌ دَسَرَهُ الْبَحْرُ لَيْسَ فِيهِ شَيْءٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn ‘Uyaynah → ‘Amr → Ibn Udhaynah → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Abū Bakr told us: Ibn ‘Uyaynah told us, from ‘Amr, from Ibn Udhaynah, who heard Ibn ‘Abbās say: “Ambergris is not Rikāz (buried treasure); rather, it is something the sea has cast out. There is nothing (Zakat) in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ ابْنِ أُذَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْعَنْبَرِ زَكَاةٌ، إِنَّمَا هُوَ شَيْءٌ دَسَرَهُ الْبَحْرُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān al-Thawrī → ‘Amr → Ibn Udhaynah → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān al-Thawrī, from ‘Amr, from Ibn Udhaynah, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: “There is no Zakat in Ambergris; it is only something the sea has cast out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْعَنْبَرِ زَكَاةٌ، إِنَّمَا هُوَ غَنِيمَةٌ لِمَنْ أَخَذَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ibrāhīm ibn Ismā‘īl → Abū al-Zubayr → Jābir
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ibrāhīm ibn Ismā‘īl, from Abū al-Zubayr, from Jābir, who said: “There is no Zakat in Ambergris; it is only booty for the one who takes it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، أَنَّ عُرْوَةَ بْنَ مُحَمَّدٍ، كَتَبَ إِلَى عُمَرَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ فِي عَنْبَرَةٍ فِيهَا سَبْعُمِائَةِ رِطْلٍ فَقَالَ: فِيهَا الْخُمُسُ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Ma‘mar → ‘Urwah ibn Muḥammad → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah told us, from Ma‘mar, that ‘Urwah ibn Muḥammad wrote to ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz regarding a piece of ambergris weighing seven hundred ratls. He said: “In it is the fifth (Khums).”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ خَمَّسَ الْعَنْبَرَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Layth → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Layth, that ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz took the fifth from ambergris.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُعَاذُ بْنُ مُعَاذٍ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: كَانَ يَقُولُ: "فِي الْعَنْبَرِ الْخُمُسُ، وَكَذَلِكَ كَانَ يَقُولُ: فِي اللُّؤْلُؤِ

english.narrator: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh → Ash‘ath → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Mu‘ādh ibn Mu‘ādh told us, from Ash‘ath, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: He used to say: “In ambergris, there is the fifth.” And he also used to say the same regarding pearls.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنَ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ الْعَنْبَرِ، فَقَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ فِيهِ شَيْءٌ فَفِيهِ الْخُمُسُ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Ibn Ṭāwūs → His Father → Ibrāhīm ibn Sa‘d ibn ‘Ayyāsh
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah told us, from Ibn Ṭāwūs, from his father, who said: I asked Ibrāhīm ibn Sa‘d ibn ‘Ayyāsh about ambergris. He said: “If there is anything in it, then it is the fifth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ الثَّوْرِيِّ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّ ابْنَ عَبَّاسٍ، سُئِلَ عَنْ الْعَنْبَرِ فَقَالَ: إِنْ كَانَ فِيهِ شَيْءٌ فَفِيهِ الْخُمُسُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān al-Thawrī → Ibn Ṭāwūs → His Father → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān al-Thawrī, from Ibn Ṭāwūs, from his father, that Ibn ‘Abbās was asked about ambergris. He said: “If there is anything in it, then it is the fifth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، قَالَ: كَانَ سُفْيَانُ، يَقُولُ: لَيْسَ فِي الْعَنْبَرِ، وَلَا فِي الْعَسَلِ، وَلَا فِي الْأَوْقَاصِ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān
english.text: Wakī‘ said: Sufyān used to say: “There is no Zakat in ambergris, nor in honey, nor in Awqāṣ.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا فِي اللُّؤْلُؤِ، وَالزُّمُرُّدِ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ خُصَيْفٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي حَجَرِ اللُّؤْلُؤِ، وَلَا حَجَرِ الزُّمُرُّدِ زَكَاةٌ، إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَا لِتِجَارَةٍ، فَإِنْ كَانَا لِتِجَارَةٍ فَفِيهِمَا زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Khuṣayf → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Abū Bakr told us: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ told us, from Khuṣayf, from ‘Ikrimah, who said: “There is no Zakat in pearls or emerald stones, unless they are for trade. If they are for trade, then there is Zakat in them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَرَزِ وَاللُّؤْلُؤِ زَكَاةٌ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَا لِتِجَارَةٍ.

english.narrator: Sharīk → Sālim → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Sharīk told us, from Sālim, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: “There is no Zakat in beads and pearls unless they are for trade.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ خُصَيْفٍ، عَنْ عِكْرِمَةَ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Khuṣayf → ‘Ikrimah
english.text: Sharīk told us, from Khuṣayf, from ‘Ikrimah, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَرَزِ زَكَاةٌ إِلَّا لِتِجَارَةٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Sālim → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Sālim, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: “There is no Zakat in beads unless they are for trade.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، وَالزُّهْرِيِّ، وَمَكْحُولِ، قَالُوا: لَيْسَ فِي الْجَوْهَرِ شَيْءٌ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ لِتِجَارَةٍ

english.narrator: Ibn Numayr → Ḥajjāj → ‘Aṭā’ + Al-Zuhrī + Makḥūl
english.text: Ibn Numayr told us, from Ḥajjāj, from ‘Aṭā’, Al-Zuhrī, and Makḥūl, who said: “There is nothing in jewelry (gems) unless it is for trade.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سَهْلُ بْنُ يُوسُفَ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى فِي الْحُلِيِّ زَكَاةً إِلَّا فِي الذَّهَبِ، وَالْفِضَّةِ، وَلَا يَرَاهُ فِي الْجَوْهَرِ، وَاللُّؤْلُؤِ، وَهَذَا النَّحْوِ

english.narrator: Sahl ibn Yūsuf → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Sahl ibn Yūsuf told us, from Shu‘bah, from Al-Ḥakam, that he used to not see Zakat in jewelry except in gold and silver, and he did not see it in gems, pearls, and the like.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ طَلْحَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ: "كُلُّ شَيْءٍ أُرِيدَ بِهِ التِّجَارَةُ فَفِيهِ الزَّكَاةُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ لَبَنٌ، أَوْ طِينٌ قَالَ: وَكَانَ الْحَكَمُ يَرَى ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → Ḥajjāj → Ṭalḥah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Ḥafṣ told us, from Ḥajjāj, from Ṭalḥah, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “Everything that is intended for trade has Zakat in it, even if it is bricks or clay.” He said: And Al-Ḥakam used to hold that view.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْجَوْهَرِ زَكَاةٌ، إِلَّا أَنْ يُشْتَرَى لِتِجَارَةٍ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Mughīrah → Ḥammād
english.text: Jarīr told us, from Mughīrah, from Ḥammād, who said: “There is no Zakat in gems unless they are bought for trade.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بُكَيْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ لِي عَطَاءٌ: لَا صَدَقَةَ فِي اللُّؤْلُؤِ، وَلَا زَبَرْجَدٍ، وَلَا يَاقُوتٍ، وَلَا فُصُوصٍ، وَلَا عَرْضٍ، وَلَا شَيْءٍ لَا يُدَارُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ شَيْئًا مِنْ ذَلِكَ يُدَارُ فَفِيهِ الصَّدَقَةُ فِي ثَمَنِهِ حِينَ يُبَاعُ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bukayr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bukayr told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: ‘Aṭā’ said to me: “There is no Sadaqah in pearls, nor chrysolite, nor rubies, nor gemstones, nor merchandise, nor anything that is not turned over (traded). But if any of that is traded, then there is Sadaqah in its price when it is sold.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَعْفَرُ بْنُ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ أُسَامَةَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْقَاسِمَ عَنْ اللُّؤْلُؤِ، هَلْ فِيهِ زَكَاةٌ أَمْ لَا؟ فَقَالَ: مَا كَانَ مِنْهُ يُلْبَسُ كَالْحُلِيِّ لَيْسَ لِتِجَارَةٍ فَلَا زَكَاةَ فِيهِ، وَمَا كَانَ مِنْ ذَلِكَ لِلتِّجَارَةِ فَفِيهِ الزَّكَاةُ

english.narrator: Ja‘far ibn ‘Awn → Usāmah → Al-Qāsim
english.text: Ja‘far ibn ‘Awn told us, from Usāmah, who said: I asked Al-Qāsim about pearls: Is there Zakat in them or not? He said: “Whatever of it is worn as jewelry and is not for trade, there is no Zakat in it. And whatever of it is for trade, then there is Zakat in it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَزْهَرُ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنْ أَبِي الْمَلِيحِ، فِي حَدِيثٍ ذَكَرَهُ كَأَنَّهُ يَرَى فِيهِ الزَّكَاةَ، يَعْنِي اللُّؤْلُؤَ،

english.narrator: Azhar → Ibn ‘Awn → Abū al-Malīḥ
english.text: Azhar told us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Abū al-Malīḥ, in a Hadith he mentioned, as if he saw Zakat in it—meaning pearls.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَا قَالُوا فِيمَا سُقِيَ سَيْحًا وَبِالدَّوَالِي حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنْ عَبْدِ الْكَرِيمِ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، عَنِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ قَالَ: مَا سُقِيَ سَيْحًا فَفِيهِ الْعُشْرُ، وَمَا سُقِيَ بِالْغُرْبِ فَفِيهِ نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Alī ibn Hāshim → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Abd al-Karīm → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb → His Father → His Grandfather
english.text: Abū Bakr told us: ‘Alī ibn Hāshim told us, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from ‘Abd al-Karīm, from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, from the Prophet ﷺ, who said: “What is watered by flowing water has the tenth, and what is watered by the large bucket has half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ الْأَجْلَحِ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِيهِ، قَالَ: كَتَبَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِلَى الْيَمَنِ يُؤْخَذُ مِمَّا سَقَتِ السَّمَاءُ، وَسُقِيَ بِالْغَيْلِ مِنَ الْحِنْطَةِ، وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالزَّبِيبِ الْعُشْرُ، وَمَا سُقِيَ بِالسَّوَانِي نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: ‘Alī ibn Mushir → Al-Ajlaḥ → Al-Sha‘bī → His Father
english.text: ‘Alī ibn Mushir told us, from Al-Ajlaḥ, from Al-Sha‘bī, from his father, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ wrote to Yemen: “From wheat, barley, dates, and raisins that are watered by the sky (rain) and watered by streams, the tenth is taken; and what is watered by camels (irrigation wheels), half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: كَتَبَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِلَى مُعَاذٍ بِالْيَمَنِ أَنَّ فِيمَا سَقَتِ السَّمَاءُ أَوْ سُقِيَ غَيْلًا الْعُشْرَ، وَفِيمَا سُقِيَ بِالْغُرْبِ، وَالدَّالِيَةِ نِصْفَ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Manṣūr → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Jarīr told us, from Manṣūr, from Al-Ḥakam, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ wrote to Mu‘ādh in Yemen that: “In what is watered by the sky or watered by streams, there is the tenth; and in what is watered by the bucket and the waterwheel, there is half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ هَمَّامٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ صَالِحٍ أَبِي الْخَلِيلِ، قَالَ: سَنَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِيمَا سَقَتِ السَّمَاءُ أَوِ الْعَيْنُ السَّائِحَةُ، وَمَاءُ الْغَيْلِ، أَوْ كَانَ بَعْلًا الْعُشْرُ كَامِلًا، وَمَا سُقِيَ بِالرِّشَاءِ الْحَبْلِ فَنِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Hammām → Qatādah → Ṣāliḥ Abū al-Khalīl
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Hammām, from Qatādah, from Ṣāliḥ Abū al-Khalīl, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ established the sunnah regarding what is watered by the sky, or a flowing spring, or stream water, or is Ba‘l (watered by roots reaching underground water), the full tenth; and what is watered by the rope (bucket/labor), half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ ضَمْرَةَ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: فِيمَا سَقَتِ السَّمَاءُ أَوْ كَانَ سَيْحًا فِيهَا الْعُشْرُ، وَمَا سُقِيَ بِالدَّالِيَةِ فَنِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Abū Isḥāq → ‘Āṣim ibn Ḍamrah → ‘Alī
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Abū Isḥāq, from ‘Āṣim ibn Ḍamrah, from ‘Alī, who said: “In what is watered by the sky or is flowing water, there is the tenth; and what is watered by the waterwheel, there is half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عَرُوبَةَ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، قَالَ: سَنَّ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِيمَا سَقَتِ السَّمَاءُ أَوْ سَقَى الْغَيْلُ وَكَانَ بَعْلًا الْعُشْرُ كَامِلًا، وَمَا سُقِيَ بِالرِّشَاءِ فَنِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ قَالَ: وَقَالَ قَتَادَةُ وَكَانَ يُقَالُ: فِيمَا يُكَالُ مِنَ الثَّمَرَةِ الْعُشْرُ، وَنِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ibn Abī ‘Arūbah → Qatādah
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah told us, from Ibn Abī ‘Arūbah, from Qatādah, who said: “The Messenger of Allah ﷺ established the sunnah regarding what is watered by the sky or watered by streams and was Ba‘l, the full tenth; and what is watered by the rope (bucket), half of the tenth.” He said: And Qatādah said—and it used to be said: “In what is measured of fruit, there is the tenth and half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي مُوسَى بْنُ عُقْبَةَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: صَدَقَةُ الثِّمَارِ، وَالزَّرْعِ، وَمَا كَانَ مِنْ نَخْلٍ، أَوْ زَرْعٍ مِنْ حِنْطَةٍ، أَوْ شَعِيرٍ، أَوْ سُلْتٍ مِمَّا كَانَ بَعْلًا، أَوْ يُسْقَى بِنَهْرٍ، أَوْ يُسْقَى بِالْعَيْنِ، أَوْ عَثَرِيًّا، وَمَا يُسْقَى بِالْمَطَرِ فَفِيهِ الْعُشْرُ مِنْ كُلِّ عَشَرَةٍ وَاحِدٌ، وَمَا كَانَ مِنْهُ يُسْقَى بِالنَّضْحِ فَفِيهِ نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ، وَفِي كُلِّ عِشْرِينَ وَاحِدٌ، وَكَتَبَ النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِلَى أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ إِلَى الْحَارِثِ بْنِ عَبْدِ كَلَالٍ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ مِنْ مَعَافِرَ وَهَمْدَانَ: إِنَّ عَلَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنْ صَدَقَةِ أَمْوَالِهِمْ عُشُورَ مَا سَقَتِ الْعَيْنُ وَسَقَتِ السَّمَاءُ الْعُشْرُ، وَعَلَى مَا يُسْقَى بِالْغُرْبِ نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → Mūsā ibn ‘Uqbah → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: Mūsā ibn ‘Uqbah informed me, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, that he used to say: The Sadaqah of fruits and crops—whatever date palms or crops of wheat, barley, or Sult (a type of barley) that was Ba‘l, or watered by a river, or watered by a spring, or rain-fed (‘Atharī), and what is watered by rain—in it is the tenth, one out of every ten. And whatever of it is watered by camel-irrigation (Naḍḥ), in it is half of the tenth, one out of every twenty. And the Prophet ﷺ wrote to the people of Yemen, to Al-Ḥārith ibn ‘Abd Kulāl and those with him from the people of Yemen from Ma‘āfir and Hamdān: “Upon the believers regarding the Sadaqah of their wealth: the tithes of what is watered by the spring and watered by the sky is the tenth; and upon what is watered by the bucket is half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: فِيمَا يُسْقَى بِالْكَظَائِمِ مِنْ نَخْلٍ، أَوْ عِنَبٍ، أَوْ حَبٍّ قَالَ: الْعُشْرُ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: I said to ‘Aṭā’: Regarding what is watered by underground channels (Kaẓā’im) of palm trees, grapes, or grain? He said: “The tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، أَنَّهُ سَمِعَهُ يَقُولُ: فِيهَا الْعُشْرُ قُلْتُ: فِيمَا يُسْقَى غَيْلًا مِنْ نَخْلٍ، أَوْ عِنَبٍ، أَوْ حَبٍّ، قَالَ: الْعُشْرُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ: فِيمَا يُسْقَى بِالدَّلْوِ وَبِالْمَنَاضِحِ قَالَ: نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → Abū al-Zubayr → Jābir
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: Abū al-Zubayr informed me, from Jābir, that he heard him say: “In it is the tenth.” I said: Regarding what is watered by streams of palm trees, grapes, or grain? He said: “The tenth.” He said: I said: Regarding what is watered by the bucket and by camels? He said: “Half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي أَبُو الزُّبَيْرِ قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي جَابِرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ: نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → Abū al-Zubayr → Jābir ibn ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: Abū al-Zubayr informed me: Jābir ibn ‘Abd Allāh informed me that he said: “Half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُوَقِّتُ فِي الثَّمَرَةِ شَيْئًا، وَيَقُولُ: الْعُشْرُ وَنِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ.

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Ma‘mar → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Ma‘mar, from Al-Zuhrī, that he used to not set a specific threshold for fruit, and he would say: “The tenth and half of the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنْ رَجُلٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، مِثْلَهُ.

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Ma‘mar → A Man → Mujāhid
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Ma‘mar, from a man, from Mujāhid, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْأَعْلَى، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، قَالَ: كَتَبَ بِذَلِكَ عُمَرُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ إِلَى أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-A‘lā → Ma‘mar → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz
english.text: ‘Abd al-A‘lā told us, from Ma‘mar, who said: ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz wrote that to the people of Yemen.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: فِي الْبُرِّ، وَالشَّعِيرِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَالْعِنَبِ، إِذَا كَانَ خَمْسَةَ أَوْسَاقٍ، وَذَلِكَ ثَلَاثِمِائَةِ صَاعٍ فَفِيهِ نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ إِذَا كَانَ يُسْقَى، وَمَا سَقَتِ السَّمَاءُ، وَالْعَيْنُ فَفِيهِ الْعُشْرُ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Wahhāb → ‘Aṭā’ → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: ‘Abd al-Wahhāb told us, from ‘Aṭā’, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “In wheat, barley, dates, and grapes, if it is five Awsuq—and that is three hundred Ṣā‘—then in it is half of the tenth if it is irrigated (by labor), and what the sky and spring water, in it is the tenth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَبَابَةُ بْنُ سَوَّارٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ سَعْدٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ، كَانَ يُفْتِي فِي صَدَقَةِ الزَّرْعِ، وَالثِّمَارِ، وَمَا كَانَ فِيهِمَا يَشْرَبُ بِالنَّهْرِ، أَوِ الْعَيْنِ، أَوْ عَثَرِيًّا أَوْ بَعْلٍ فَإِنَّ صَدَقَةَ الْعُشُورِ مِنْ كُلِّ عَشَرَةٍ وَاحِدٌ، وَمَا كَانَ مِنْهَا بِالْإِنْضَاحِ، فَإِنَّ صَدَقَتَهُ نِصْفُ الْعُشُورِ فِي كُلِّ عِشْرِينَ وَاحِدٌ

english.narrator: Shabābah ibn Sawwār → Layth → Sa‘d → Nāfi‘ → ‘Abd Allāh
english.text: Shabābah ibn Sawwār told us, from Layth, from Sa‘d, from Nāfi‘, that ‘Abd Allāh used to give fatwas regarding the Sadaqah of crops and fruits: whatever of them drinks from a river, or a spring, or is rain-fed, or Ba‘l, “Then the Sadaqah of tithes is one out of every ten. And whatever of them is by camel-irrigation, then its Sadaqah is half of the tithes, one out of every twenty.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَا قَالُوا فِيمَا يُسْقَى سَيْحًا، وَيُسْقَى بِالدَّلْوِ كَيْفَ يُصَدَّقُ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الزَّرْعِ يَكُونُ عَلَى سَيْحِ الزَّمَانِ، ثُمَّ يُسْقَى بِالْبِئْرِ، يَعْنِي بِالدَّلْوِ، وَبِالدَّالِيَةِ قَالَ: يُصَدَّقُ عَلَى أَكْثَرِ ذَلِكَ أَنْ يُسْقَى بِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Mubārak → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Abū Bakr told us: Ibn Mubārak told us, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Aṭā’, regarding crops that are on flowing water for a time, then watered by the well—meaning by bucket and waterwheel. He said: “Sadaqah is paid based on the majority of that by which it is watered.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: إِنَّمَا يَكُونُ عَلَى الْعَيْنِ عَامَّةَ الزَّمَانَ، ثُمَّ يَحْتَاجُ إِلَى الْبِئْرِ فِي الْقِطَعِ يَسْقِي بِهَا، ثُمَّ الْقِطْعَةِ، ثُمَّ يَصِيرُ إِلَى الْعَيْنِ كَيْفَ صَدَقَتُهُ؟ قَالَ: الْعُشْرُ، وَقَدْ يَكُونُ ذَلِكَ عَلَى أَكْثَرِ ذَلِكَ أَنْ يَسْقِيَ بِهِ، إِنْ كَانَ يَسْقِي بِالْعَيْنِ أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا يَسْقِي بِالدَّلْوِ فَفِيهِ الْعُشْرُ، وَإِنْ كَانَ يَسْقِي بِالدَّلْوِ أَكْثَرَ مِمَّا يَسْقِي بِالْعَيْنِ فَفِيهِ نِصْفُ الْعُشْرِ، قُلْتُ: هُوَ بِمَنْزِلَةِ ذَلِكَ أَيْضًا الْمَالُ يَكُونُ بَعْلًا، أَوْ عَثَرِيًّا عَامَّةَ الزَّمَانِ، ثُمَّ يَحْتَاجُ إِلَى الْبِئْرِ قَالَ: نَعَمْ. قَالَ أَبُو الزُّبَيْرِ، وَسَمِعْتُ ابْنَ عُمَرَ، يَقُولُ: هَذَا الْقَوْلَ. ثُمَّ سَأَلْتُ سَالِمَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ: مِثْلَ قَوْلِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr told us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: I said to ‘Aṭā’: It is on the spring for most of the time, then he needs the well for the plot to water with it, then the plot, then it goes back to the spring; how is its Sadaqah? He said: “The tenth. And that may be based on the majority of what he waters with. If he waters with the spring more than he waters with the bucket, then in it is the tenth. And if he waters with the bucket more than he waters with the spring, then in it is half of the tenth.” I said: Is it in the same status as wealth being Ba‘l or rain-fed for most of the time, then needing the well? He said: “Yes.” Abū al-Zubayr said: And I heard Ibn ‘Umar saying this statement. Then I asked Sālim ibn ‘Abd Allāh, and he said similar to the statement of ‘Abd Allāh.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَا قَالُوا فِي الرَّجُلِ يُخْرِجُ زَكَاةَ أَرْضِهِ وَقَدْ أَنْفَقَ فِي الْبُذُورِ وَالْبَقَرِ حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الْوَهَّابِ الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ حَبِيبٍ الْمُعَلِّمِ، قَالَ: كَانَ عَطَاءٌ، يَقُولُ: فِي الزَّرْعِ إِذَا أَعْطَى صَاحِبُهُ أَجْرَ الْحَصَّادِينَ وَالَّذِينَ يُدَوِّرُونَ هَلْ عَلَيْهِ فِيمَا أَعْطَاهُمْ صَدَقَةٌ؟ قَالَ: لَا إِنَّمَا الصَّدَقَةُ فِيمَا حَصَلَ فِي يَدِكَ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Wahhāb al-Thaqafī → Ḥabīb al-Mu‘allim → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: ‘Abd al-Wahhāb al-Thaqafī told us, from Ḥabīb al-Mu‘allim, who said: ‘Aṭā’ used to say regarding crops, if its owner gives the wages of the harvesters and those who thresh/winnow: Is there Sadaqah upon him in what he gave them? He said: “No, Sadaqah is only in what is obtained in your hand.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أَبِي عَوَانَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي بِشْرٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ هَرِمٍ، عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، وَابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ فِي الرَّجُلِ يُنْفِقُ عَلَى ثَمَرَتِهِ فَقَالَ: أَحَدُهُمَا يُزَكِّيهَا وَقَالَ الْآخَرُ: يَرْفَعُ النَّفَقَةَ وَيُزَكِّي مَا بَقِيَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Abū ‘Awānah → Abū Bishr → ‘Amr ibn Harim → Jābir ibn Zayd → Ibn ‘Umar + Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Abū ‘Awānah, from Abū Bishr, from ‘Amr ibn Harim, from Jābir ibn Zayd, from Ibn ‘Umar and Ibn ‘Abbās regarding a man who spends on his fruit (cultivation costs). One of them said: “He pays Zakat on it (the gross).” And the other said: “He deducts the expenses and pays Zakat on what remains.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْمَلِكِ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: ارْفَعْ الْبَذْرَ، وَالنَّفَقَةَ، وَزَكِّ مَا بَقِيَ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Abd al-Malik → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Abd al-Malik, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: “Deduct the seed and the expenses, and pay Zakat on what remains.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مَا قَالُوا فِي تَعْجِيلِ الزَّكَاةِ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصُ بْنُ غِيَاثٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، أَنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ بَعَثَ سَاعِيًا عَلَى الصَّدَقَةِ فَأَتَى الْعَبَّاسَ يَتَسَلَّفُهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْعَبَّاسُ: إِنِّي أَسْلَفْتُ صَدَقَةَ مَالِي سَنَتَيْنِ فَأَتَى النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فَقَالَ: صَدَقَ عَمِّي

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth → Ḥajjāj → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Abū Bakr told us: Ḥafṣ ibn Ghiyāth told us, from Ḥajjāj, from Al-Ḥakam, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent a collector for Sadaqah, and he came to Al-‘Abbās asking him to pay in advance. Al-‘Abbās said to him: “I have paid the Sadaqah of my wealth for two years in advance.” So he came to the Prophet ﷺ (and told him), and he said: “My uncle spoke the truth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ يُوسُفَ بْنِ عَبْدَةَ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يُعَجِّلَهَا

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Yūsuf ibn ‘Abdah → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd told us, from Yūsuf ibn ‘Abdah, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: “There is no harm in hastening it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِتَعْجِيلِ الزَّكَاةِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Sālim → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Wakī‘ told us, from Sufyān, from Sālim, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: “There is no harm in hastening Zakat.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، أَوْ عَنْ حَمَّادٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ تُعَجِّلَ زَكَاةَ مَالِكَ، وَتَحْتَسِبَ بِهَا فِيمَا يَسْتَقْبِلُ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm, Ḥammād
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, or from Ḥammād, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “There is no harm if you pay the Zakat of your wealth in advance and count it towards what is to come.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةُ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ بِتَعْجِيلِ الزَّكَاةِ إِذَا أَخْرَجهَا جَمِيعًا

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “There is no harm in paying Zakat in advance if he gives it all out.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ حَفْصِ بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الْحَسَنَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ، أَخْرَجَ زَكَاةَ ثَلَاثَ سِنِينَ ضَرْبَةً قَالَ: يُجْزِيهِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ḥammād ibn Zayd → Ḥafṣ ibn Sulaymān → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ḥammād ibn Zayd, from Ḥafṣ ibn Sulaymān, who said: I asked Al-Ḥasan about a man who paid Zakat for three years all at once. He said: “It suffices him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ زَيْدٍ، عَنْ جُوَيْبِرٍ، عَنِ الضَّحَّاكِ، قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يُعَجِّلَهَا، قَبْلَ مَحِلِّهَا

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Zayd → Juwaybir → Al-Ḍaḥḥāk
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Zayd narrated to us, from Juwaybir, from Al-Ḍaḥḥāk, who said: “There is no harm if he pays it in advance, before its due time.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حُمَيْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ قَالَ: لَا بَأْسَ أَنْ يُعَجِّلَهَا

english.narrator: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān → Al-Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ → Ibn Abī Laylā → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Ḥumayd ibn ‘Abd al-Raḥmān narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan ibn Ṣāliḥ, from Ibn Abī Laylā, from Al-Ḥakam, who said: “There is no harm if he pays it in advance.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عُمَرُ بْنُ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى بَأْسًا أَنْ يُعَجِّلَ الرَّجُلُ زَكَاتَهُ قَبْلَ الْحِلِّ

english.narrator: ‘Umar ibn Yūnus → Al-Zuhrī
english.text: ‘Umar ibn Yūnus narrated to us, from Al-Zuhrī, “That he saw no harm in a man paying his Zakat in advance before it is due.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَوْنٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: مَا أَدْرِي مَا هَذَا فِي تَعْجِيلِ الزَّكَاةِ قَبْلَ الْحِلِّ بِشَهْرٍ، أَوْ شَهْرَيْنِ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Ibn ‘Awn → Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Ibn ‘Awn, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: “I do not know about this paying Zakat in advance before it is due by a month or two months.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ مَعْمَرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُخْرِجُ لَهُ الطَّعَامَ مِنْ أَرْضِهِ فَيُزَكِّيهِ، ثُمَّ يَمْكُثُ عِنْدَهُ السَّنَتَيْنِ، وَالثَّلَاثَ فَلَا يُزَكِّيهِ، وَهُوَ يُرِيدُ أَنْ يَبِيعَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Mubārak → Ma‘mar → Ibn Ṭāwūs → His Father
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Mubārak narrated to us, from Ma‘mar, from Ibn Ṭāwūs, from his father, “That food would be produced for him from his land and he would pay Zakat on it, then it would remain with him for two or three years and he would not pay Zakat on it, while intending to sell it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ لَهِيعَةَ، قَالَ: حَدَّثَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، كَتَبَ إِذَا أُخِذَ مِنَ الزَّرْعِ الْعُشْرُ فَلَيْسَ فِيهِ زَكَاةٌ، إِنْ مَكَثَ عَشْرَ سِنِينَ

english.narrator: Ibn Mubārak → Ibn Lahī‘ah → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Ja‘far → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz
english.text: Ibn Mubārak narrated to us, from Ibn Lahī‘ah, who said: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Ja‘far narrated to me, that ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz wrote: “If the tithe (Ushr) is taken from the crops, there is no [further] Zakat on it, even if it remains for ten years.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ السَّلَامِ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: إِذَا أَخْرَجَ صَدَقَةَ الزَّرْعِ، وَالتَّمْرِ، وَكُلِّ شَيْءٍ أَنْبَتَتِ الْأَرْضُ فَلَيْسَ فِيهِ زَكَاةٌ حَتَّى يَحُولَ عَلَيْهِ الْحَوْلُ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Salām → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: ‘Abd al-Salām narrated to us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “If he pays the charity on crops, dates, and everything the earth produces, there is no Zakat on it until a year passes over it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِعَطَاءٍ: طَعَامٌ أُمْسِكُهُ أُرِيدُ أَكْلَهُ فَيَحُولُ عَلَيْهِ الْحَوْلُ قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ فِيهِ صَدَقَةٌ لَعَمْرِي، إِنَّا لَنَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ نَبْتَاعُ الطَّعَامَ وَمَا نُزَكِّيهِ، فَإِنْ كُنْتَ تُرِيدُ بَيْعَهُ فَزَكِّهِ إِذَا بِعْتَهُ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: I said to ‘Aṭā’: “Food that I keep, intending to eat it, and a year passes over it.” He said: “There is no charity due on it from you. By my life, we do that; we buy food and do not pay Zakat on it. But if you intend to sell it, then pay Zakat on it when you sell it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ قَالَ: قَالَ لِي عَبْدُ الْكَرِيمِ فِي الْحَرْثِ: إِذَا أَعْطَيْتَ زَكَاتَهُ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ فَحَالَ عَلَيْهِ الْحَوْلُ عِنْدَكَ، فَلَا تُزَكِّهِ حَسْبُكَ الْأُولَى

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Abd al-Karīm
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: ‘Abd al-Karīm said to me regarding crops: “If you gave its Zakat the first time and a year passes over it while it is with you, do not pay Zakat on it. The first time is sufficient for you.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ أَبِي الْيَقْظَانِ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي لَيْلَى، أَنَّ عَلِيًّا، زَكَّى أَمْوَالَ بَنِي أَبِي رَافِعٍ أَيْتَامٌ فِي حِجْرِهِ وَقَالَ: تَرَوْنَ كُنْتُ أَلِي مَالًا لَا أُزَكِّيهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Sharīk → Abū al-Yaqẓān → Ibn Abī Laylā → ‘Alī
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Sharīk narrated to us, from Abū al-Yaqẓān, from Ibn Abī Laylā, that ‘Alī paid Zakat on the wealth of the sons of Abū Rāfi‘, orphans under his care, and said: “Do you think I would manage wealth and not pay Zakat on it?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، قَالَ: كُنَّا أَيْتَامًا فِي حِجْرِ عَائِشَةَ فَكَانَتْ تُزَكِّي أَمْوَالَنَا، وَتُبْضِعْنِيهَا فِي الْبَحْرِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Alī ibn Mushir → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → Al-Qāsim
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Alī ibn Mushir narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from Al-Qāsim, who said: “We were orphans under the care of ‘Ā’ishah, and she used to pay Zakat on our wealth and trade with it by sea.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ أَشْعَثَ، عَنْ أَبِي الزُّبَيْرِ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، قَالَ: فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān → Ash‘ath → Abū al-Zubayr → Jābir
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Ash‘ath, from Abū al-Zubayr, from Jābir, who said: “There is Zakat on the wealth of the orphan.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُزَكِّي مَالَ الْيَتِيمِ

english.narrator: ‘Alī ibn Mushir → Layth → Nāfi‘ → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: ‘Alī ibn Mushir narrated to us, from Layth, from Nāfi‘, from Ibn ‘Umar, “That he used to pay Zakat on the wealth of the orphan.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الزُّهْرِيِّ، قَالَ عُمَرُ: ابْتَغُوا لِلْيَتَامَى فِي أَمْوَالِهِمْ، لَا تَسْتَغْرِقُهَا الزَّكَاةُ

english.narrator: Ibn Idrīs → Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq → Al-Zuhrī → ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Muḥammad ibn Isḥāq, from Al-Zuhrī, that ‘Umar said: “Trade with the wealth of orphans so that Zakat does not consume it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو خَالِدٍ الْأَحْمَرُ، عَنْ يَحْيَى، وَحَنْظَلَةَ، وَحُمَيْدٌ، عَنِ الْقَاسِمِ، أَنَّ عَائِشَةَ كَانَتْ تُبْضِعُ أَمْوَالَهُمْ فِي الْبَحْرِ، وَتُزَكِّيهَا

english.narrator: Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar → Yaḥyā, Ḥanẓalah, Ḥumayd → Al-Qāsim → ‘Ā’ishah
english.text: Abū Khālid al-Aḥmar narrated to us, from Yaḥyā, Ḥanẓalah, and Ḥumayd, from Al-Qāsim, “That ‘Ā’ishah used to trade with their wealth by sea and pay Zakat on it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُلَيَّةَ، عَنْ أَيُّوبَ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: قَالَ عُمَرُ: ابْتَغُوا بِأَمْوَالِ الْيَتَامَى، لَا تَسْتَغْرِقُهَا الصَّدَقَةُ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Ulayyah → Ayyūb → ‘Amr ibn Dīnār → Makḥūl → ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Ulayyah narrated to us, from Ayyūb, from ‘Amr ibn Dīnār, from Makḥūl, who said: ‘Umar said: “Trade with the wealth of orphans so that charity [Zakat] does not consume it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ حُسَيْنٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي فَرْوَةَ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ḥusayn → Ibn Abī Farwah → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ḥusayn, from Ibn Abī Farwah, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “There is Zakat on the wealth of the orphan.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ سِيرِينَ، قَالَ: "فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ لَهُ حَقٌّ، وَعَلَيْهِ حَقٌّ، وَلَا أَقُولُ إِلَّا مَا قَالَ: اللَّهُ تَعَالَى

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām → Ibn Sīrīn
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām, from Ibn Sīrīn, who said: “Regarding the wealth of the orphan, he has a right [to receive] and a right [obligation] upon him, and I say nothing except what Allah Almighty said.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ يَزِيدَ، عَنْ طَاوُسٍ، قَالَ: زَكِّ مَالَ الْيَتِيمِ، وَإِلَّا فَهُوَ دَيْنٌ فِي عُنُقِكَ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Yamān → Al-Ḥasan ibn Yazīd → Ṭāwūs
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Yamān narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan ibn Yazīd, from Ṭāwūs, who said: “Pay Zakat on the orphan's wealth, otherwise it is a debt on your neck.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُوسَى بْنِ عُبَيْدَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، قَالَ: دَعَا ابْنُ عُمَرَ إِلَى مَالِ يَتِيمٍ فَقَالَ: إِنْ شِئْتُمْ وَلَّيْتُهُ عَلَى أَنْ أُزَكِّيَهُ حَوْلًا إِلَى حَوْلٍ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mūsā ibn ‘Ubaydah → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Mūsā ibn ‘Ubaydah, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār, who said: Ibn ‘Umar was invited to [manage] an orphan's wealth. He said: “If you wish, I will manage it on the condition that I pay Zakat on it year after year.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ نُمَيْرٍ، عَنْ مَالِكِ بْنِ مِغْوَلٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، أَنَّهُ رَأَى فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ زَكَاةً

english.narrator: Ibn Numayr → Mālik ibn Mighwal → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Ibn Numayr narrated to us, from Mālik ibn Mighwal, from ‘Aṭā’, that he “considered Zakat due on the orphan's wealth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: ثنا ابْنُ إِدْرِيسَ، عَنْ لَيْثٍ، عَنْ مُجَاهِدٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ مَسْعُودٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: أَحْصِ مَا يَجِبُ فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ مِنَ الزَّكَاةِ، فَإِذَا بَلَغَ وَأُونِسَ مِنْهُ رُشْدٌ فَادْفَعْهُ إِلَيْهِ، فَإِنْ شَاءَ زَكَّاهُ، وَإِنْ شَاءَ تَرَكَهُ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ibn Idrīs → Layth → Mujāhid → Ibn Mas‘ūd
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ibn Idrīs narrated to us, from Layth, from Mujāhid, from Ibn Mas‘ūd, that he used to say: “Calculate what is due as Zakat on the orphan's wealth. When he reaches maturity and you perceive sound judgment in him, hand it over to him. If he wishes, he pays Zakat on it, and if he wishes, he leaves it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ زَكَاةٌ حَتَّى يَحْتَلِمَ.

english.narrator: Jarīr → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “There is no Zakat on the orphan's wealth until he reaches puberty.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ الْأَعْمَشِ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، مِثْلَهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Al-A‘mash → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Al-A‘mash, from Ibrāhīm, similar to it.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ هِشَامٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ زَكَاةٌ حَتَّى يَحْتَلِمَ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Hishām → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Hishām, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “There is no Zakat on the orphan's wealth until he reaches puberty.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ عِنْدَهُ مَالٌ لِبَنِي أَخٍ لَهُ أَيْتَامٍ فَلَا يُزَكِّيهِ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, “That he had wealth belonging to his orphan nephews, and he did not pay Zakat on it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا حَفْصٌ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، عَنْ شُرَيْحٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ أَفِيهِ زَكَاةٌ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ وَلَوْ كَانَ عِنْدِي مَا زَكَّيْتُهُ

english.narrator: Ḥafṣ → Ḥajjāj → Al-Ḥakam → Shurayḥ
english.text: Ḥafṣ narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from Al-Ḥakam, from Shurayḥ, who was asked about the orphan's wealth: “Is there Zakat on it?” He said: “Yes, but if it were with me, I would not pay Zakat on it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنْ جَابِرٍ، عَنْ عَامِرٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Jābir → ‘Āmir
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Jābir, from ‘Āmir, who said: “There is no Zakat on the orphan's wealth.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَنْ مَالِ الْيَتِيمِ فِيهِ زَكَاةٌ؟ قَالَ: نَعَمْ، وَلَوْ كَانَ عِنْدِي مَا زَكَّيْتُهُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sa‘īd ibn Dīnār
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sa‘īd ibn Dīnār, who said: I asked about the orphan's wealth: “Is there Zakat on it?” He said: “Yes, but if it were with me, I would not pay Zakat on it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا يَحْيَى بْنُ يَمَانٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ زَيْدٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ مُجَاهِدًا يَقُولُ: أَحْصِهِ فَإِذَا عَلِمْتُ فَزَكِّهِ

english.narrator: Yaḥyā ibn Yamān → Al-Ḥasan ibn Zayd → Mujāhid
english.text: Yaḥyā ibn Yamān narrated to us, from Al-Ḥasan ibn Zayd, who said: I heard Mujāhid saying: “Calculate it, and when I know [it is due? or referring to reaching maturity?], pay Zakat on it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: يُؤْخَذُ مِنَ النَّخْلِ وَالْمَاشِيَةِ، وَأَمَّا الْمَالُ فَحَتَّى يَحْتَلِمَ، يَعْنِي مَالَ الْيَتِيمِ،

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “[Zakat] is taken from date palms and livestock. As for [other] wealth, [not] until he reaches puberty.” He meant the orphan's wealth.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرِ بْنُ عَيَّاشٍ، عَنْ عَاصِمٍ، أَنَّ أَبَا وَائِلٍ، كَانَ يَقُولُ: كَانَ فِي حِجْرِي يَتِيمٌ لَهُ ثَمَانِيَةُ آلَافٍ فَلَمْ أُزَكِّهَا حَتَّى لَمَّا بَلَغَ دَفَعْتُهَا إِلَيْهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh → ‘Āṣim → Abū Wā’il
english.text: Abū Bakr ibn ‘Ayyāsh narrated to us, from ‘Āṣim, that Abū Wā’il used to say: “An orphan was under my care who had eight thousand. I did not pay Zakat on it until he reached maturity, then I handed it over to him.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَمْرٍو، عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ السَّائِبِ، قَالَ: كَانَ عِنْدَ ابْنِ عُمَرَ مَالُ يَتِيمٍ فَاسْتَسْلَفَ مَالَهُ حَتَّى لَا يُؤَدِّيَ زَكَاتَهُ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → ‘Amr → ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Sā’ib → Ibn ‘Umar
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from ‘Amr, from ‘Abd al-Raḥmān ibn al-Sā’ib, who said: “Ibn ‘Umar had an orphan's wealth, so he borrowed his wealth so as not to pay Zakat on it.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا حَاتِمُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ، عَنْ خُثَيْمِ بْنِ عِرَاكٍ قَالَ: سَمِعْتُ أَبِي، يَقُولُ: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا هُرَيْرَةَ يَقُولُ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَا صَدَقَةَ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِ فِي عَبْدِهِ وَلَا فَرَسِهِ

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → Ḥātim ibn Ismā‘īl → Khuthaym ibn ‘Irāk → My Father → Abū Hurayrah
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: Ḥātim ibn Ismā‘īl narrated to us, from Khuthaym ibn ‘Irāk, who said: I heard my father say: I heard Abū Hurayrah say: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no charity [Zakat] due on a Muslim for his slave or his horse.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا سُفْيَانُ بْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ يَسَارٍ، عَنْ عِرَاكِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَنَّهُ لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِ فِي فَرَسِهِ، وَلَا عَبْدِهِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār → Sulaymān ibn Yasār → ‘Irāk ibn Mālik → Abū Hurayrah
english.text: Sufyān ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār, from Sulaymān ibn Yasār, from ‘Irāk ibn Mālik, from Abū Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no charity due on a Muslim for his horse or his slave.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، عَنْ عِرَاكِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ أَبِي هُرَيْرَةَ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْمُسْلِمِ فِي عَبْدِهِ، وَلَا فَرَسِهِ، وَلَا وَلِيدَتِهِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Usāmah → Makḥūl → ‘Irāk ibn Mālik → Abū Hurayrah
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Usāmah, from Makḥūl, from ‘Irāk ibn Mālik, from Abū Hurayrah, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “There is no charity due on a Muslim for his slave, his horse, or his slave girl.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: قَدْ تَجَاوَزْتُ لَكُمْ عَنْ صَدَقَةِ الْخَيْلِ، وَالرَّقِيقِ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Abū Isḥāq → Al-Ḥārith → ‘Alī
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Abū Isḥāq, from Al-Ḥārith, from ‘Alī, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “I have overlooked for you the charity on horses and slaves.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنِ الْحَارِثِ، عَنْ عَلِيٍّ، قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَمَّا الْخَيْلُ، وَالرَّقِيقُ فَقَدْ عَفَوْتُ عَنْ صَدَقَاتِهَا

english.narrator: Ibn Mubārak → Ḥajjāj → Abū Isḥāq → Al-Ḥārith → ‘Alī
english.text: Ibn Mubārak narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from Abū Isḥāq, from Al-Ḥārith, from ‘Alī, who said: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: “As for horses and slaves, I have pardoned [you] from their charity.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ خَالِدٍ، عَنْ شُبَيْلِ بْنِ عَوْفٍ، قَالَ: وَكَانَ أَدْرَكَ الْجَاهِلِيَّةَ قَالَ: أَمَرَ عُمَرُ بْنُ الْخَطَّابِ النَّاسَ بِالصَّدَقَةِ، فَقَالَ النَّاسُ: يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ خَيْلٌ لَنَا، وَرَقِيقٌ افْرِضْ عَلَيْنَا عَشَرَةً عَشَرَةً فَقَالَ: أَمَّا أَنَا فَلَا أَفْرِضُ ذَلِكَ عَلَيْكُمْ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān → Ibn Khālid → Shubayl ibn ‘Awf
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Ibn Khālid, from Shubayl ibn ‘Awf—who had lived during the Jahiliyyah—who said: ‘Umar ibn al-Khaṭṭāb ordered the people to give charity. The people said: “O Commander of the Faithful, we have horses and slaves; impose on us ten by ten [or a specific amount].” He said: “As for me, I will not impose that upon you.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ بَكْرٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، قَالَ: أَخْبَرَنِي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ أَبِي حُسَيْنٍ، أَنَّ ابْنَ شِهَابٍ أَخْبَرَهُ، أَنَّ عُثْمَانَ كَانَ يُصَدِّقُ الْخَيْلَ وَأَنَّ السَّائِبَ ابْنَ أُخْتِ نَمِرٍ أَخْبَرَهُ أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَأْتِي عُمَرَ بِصَدَقَةِ الْخَيْلِ

english.narrator: Muḥammad ibn Bakr → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Ḥusayn → Ibn Shihāb
english.text: Muḥammad ibn Bakr narrated to us, from Ibn Jurayj, who said: ‘Abd Allāh ibn Abī Ḥusayn informed me that Ibn Shihāb informed him that ‘Uthmān used to “collect Zakat on horses,” and that Al-Sā’ib ibn Ukht Namir informed him that he used to bring the charity of horses to ‘Umar.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنِ ابْنِ طَاوُسٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنِ ابْنِ عَبَّاسٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي فَرَسِ الْغَازِي فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → Ibn Ṭāwūs → His Father → Ibn ‘Abbās
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from Ibn Ṭāwūs, from his father, from Ibn ‘Abbās, who said: “There is no charity due on the horse of the warrior in the way of Allah.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا ابْنُ عُيَيْنَةَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، قَالَ: سُئِلَ ابْنُ الْمُسَيِّبِ فِي الْبَرَاذِينِ صَدَقَةٌ؟ قَالَ: أَوَ فِي الْخَيْلِ صَدَقَةٌ؟

english.narrator: Ibn ‘Uyaynah → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār → Ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: Ibn ‘Uyaynah narrated to us, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār, who said: Ibn al-Musayyib was asked: “Is there charity on workhorses (Barādhīn)?” He said: “Is there charity on horses?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ دِينَارٍ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ سَعِيدَ بْنَ الْمُسَيِّبِ عَنْ صَدَقَةِ الْبَرَاذِينِ فَقَالَ: لِي أَوَ فِي الْخَيْلِ صَدَقَةٌ، أَوَ فِي الْخَيْلِ صَدَقَةٌ؟

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān → Yaḥyā ibn Sulaymān → Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār → Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Yaḥyā ibn Sulaymān, from Yaḥyā ibn Sa‘īd, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Dīnār, who said: I asked Sa‘īd ibn al-Musayyib about the charity of workhorses. He said to me: “Is there charity on horses? Is there charity on horses?”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو أُسَامَةَ، عَنْ نَافِعٍ، أَنَّ عُمَرَ بْنَ عَبْدِ الْعَزِيزِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَيْلِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Abū Usāmah → Nāfi‘ → ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz
english.text: Abū Usāmah narrated to us, from Nāfi‘, that ‘Umar ibn ‘Abd al-‘Azīz said: “There is no charity on horses.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا الثَّقَفِيُّ، عَنْ بُرْدٍ، عَنْ مَكْحُولٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَيْلِ، وَلَا الرَّقِيقِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Al-Thaqafī → Burd → Makḥūl
english.text: Al-Thaqafī narrated to us, from Burd, from Makḥūl, who said: “There is no charity on horses or slaves.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَيْلِ السَّائِمَةِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mālik → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Mālik, from ‘Aṭā’, who said: “There is no charity on grazing horses.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُسْهِرٍ، عَنِ الْأَجْلَحِ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ الشَّعْبِيَّ عَنْ صَدَقَةِ الْخَيْلِ، وَالرَّقِيقِ فَقَالَ: لَيْسَ فِيهِمَا زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: ‘Alī ibn Mushir → Al-Ajlaḥ → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: ‘Alī ibn Mushir narrated to us, from Al-Ajlaḥ, who said: I asked Al-Sha‘bī about the charity of horses and slaves. He said: “There is no Zakat on them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ مُغِيرَةَ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يَرَى فِي الرَّقِيقِ، إِذَا كَانُوا لِلتِّجَارَةِ صَدَقَةً إِلَّا الْفِطْرَ، وَلَكِنْ يُقَوِّمُهُمْ فَيُؤَدِّي عَنْهُمُ الزَّكَاةَ.

english.narrator: Hushaym → Mughīrah → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Mughīrah, from Ibrāhīm, that he “did not see any charity due on slaves if they were for trade, except Sadaqat al-Fitr, but he should evaluate them and pay Zakat on them [as trade goods].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا هُشَيْمٌ، عَنْ يُونُسَ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ: مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ

english.narrator: Hushaym → Yūnus → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Hushaym narrated to us, from Yūnus, from Al-Ḥasan, that he used to say similar to that.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ ابْنِ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَيْلِ، وَالْبَرَاذِينِ، وَالْحَمِيرِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Ibn Mubārak → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Ibn Mubārak, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “There is no charity on horses, workhorses, and donkeys.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ سُفْيَانَ، عَنِ ابْنِ جُرَيْجٍ، عَنْ عَطَاءٍ، فِي الْعَبْدِ لِلتِّجَارَةِ قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ زَكَاةٌ إِلَّا الْفِطْرُ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Sufyān → Ibn Jurayj → ‘Aṭā’
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Sufyān, from Ibn Jurayj, from ‘Aṭā’, regarding the slave for trade. He said: “There is no Zakat on him except the Fitr [charity].”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ، عَنِ ابْنِ سَالِمٍ، عَنِ الشَّعْبِيِّ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ عَلَى الْبَهِيمَةِ، وَلَا عَلَى الْمَمْلُوكِ زَكَاةٌ، إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ لِلتِّجَارَةِ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm → Ibn Sālim → Al-Sha‘bī
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm narrated to us, from Ibn Sālim, from Al-Sha‘bī, who said: “There is no Zakat on livestock or slaves unless they are for trade.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا غُنْدَرٌ، عَنْ شُعْبَةَ، عَنِ الْحَكَمِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْخَيْلِ صَدَقَةٌ. قَالَ حَمَّادٌ: فِيهَا

english.narrator: Ghundar → Shu‘bah → Al-Ḥakam
english.text: Ghundar narrated to us, from Shu‘bah, from Al-Ḥakam, who said: “There is no charity [Zakat] on horses.” Ḥammād said: “There is [Zakat] on them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ عَنْ الْحَمِيرِ فِيهَا زَكَاةٌ أَمْ لَا؟ قَالَ: أَمَّا أَنَا فَأُشَبِّهُهَا بِالْبَقَرِ، وَلَا نَعْلَمُ فِيهَا شَيْئًا

english.narrator: Jarīr → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: I asked about donkeys, is there Zakat on them or not? He said: “As for me, I liken them to cattle, but we do not know of anything regarding them.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنِ مُبَارَكٍ، عَنِ الْحَسَنِ، قَالَ: لَيْسَ فِي الْحَمِيرِ صَدَقَةٌ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Mubārak → Al-Ḥasan
english.text: Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Mubārak, from Al-Ḥasan, who said: “There is no charity on donkeys.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو بَكْرٍ قَالَ: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ بْنُ سُلَيْمَانَ، عَنْ حَجَّاجٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ أَبِيهِ، عَنْ جَدِّهِ، أَنَّ امْرَأَتَيْنِ أَتَتَا النَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَفِي أَيْدِيهِمَا أَسْوِرَةٌ مِنَ الذَّهَبِ، فَقَالَ لَهُمَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: أَتُحِبَّانِ أَنْ يُسَوِّرَكُمَا رَبُّكُمَا بِأَسْوِرَةٍ مِنْ نَارٍ؟ قَالَتَا: لَا، قَالَ: فَأَدِّيَا حَقَّ هَذَا الَّذِي فِي أَيْدِيكُمَا

english.narrator: Abū Bakr → ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān → Ḥajjāj → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb → His Father → His Grandfather
english.text: Abū Bakr narrated to us, saying: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm ibn Sulaymān narrated to us, from Ḥajjāj, from ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb, from his father, from his grandfather, that two women came to the Prophet ﷺ wearing gold bracelets on their hands. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to them: “Would you like your Lord to adorn you with bracelets of fire?” They said: “No.” He said: “Then fulfill the right of this which is on your hands.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدُ الرَّحِيمِ، وَوَكِيعٌ، عَنْ مُسَاوِرٍ الْوَرَّاقِ، عَنْ شُعَيْبٍ قَالَ: كَتَبَ عُمَرُ إِلَى أَبِي مُوسَى أَنْ اؤْمُرْ مَنْ قِبَلَكَ مِنْ نِسَاءِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ، أَنْ يُصَدِّقْنَ مِنْ حُلِيِّهِنَّ، وَلَا يَجْعَلْنَ الْهَدِيَّةَ، وَالزِّيَادَةَ تَعَارُضًا بَيْنَهُنَّ

english.narrator: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm, Wakī‘ → Musāwir al-Warrāq → Shu‘ayb
english.text: ‘Abd al-Raḥīm and Wakī‘ narrated to us, from Musāwir al-Warrāq, from Shu‘ayb, who said: ‘Umar wrote to Abū Mūsā: “Order the Muslim women near you to give charity from their jewelry, and let them not make gifts and increases a means of competition among themselves.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا عَبْدَةُ، عَنْ سَعِيدٍ، عَنْ قَتَادَةَ، عَنْ أَنَسٍ، قَالَ: يُزَكِّي مَرَّةً

english.narrator: ‘Abdah → Sa‘īd → Qatādah → Anas
english.text: ‘Abdah narrated to us, from Sa‘īd, from Qatādah, from Anas, who said: “He pays Zakat once.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْأَحْوَصِ، عَنْ أَبِي إِسْحَاقَ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ شَدَّادٍ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَرَى فِي الْحُلِيِّ زَكَاةً

english.narrator: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ → Abū Isḥāq → ‘Abd Allāh ibn Shaddād
english.text: Abū al-Aḥwaṣ narrated to us, from Abū Isḥāq, from ‘Abd Allāh ibn Shaddād, that he “used to see Zakat due on jewelry.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا جَرِيرٌ، عَنْ مَنْصُورٍ، عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ، قَالَ: فِي الْحُلِيِّ زَكَاةٌ

english.narrator: Jarīr → Manṣūr → Ibrāhīm
english.text: Jarīr narrated to us, from Manṣūr, from Ibrāhīm, who said: “There is Zakat on jewelry.”

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا شَرِيكٌ، عَنْ سَالِمٍ، عَنْ سَعِيدِ بْنِ جُبَيْرٍ، قَالَ: فِي حُلِيِّ الذَّهَبِ وَالْفِضَّةِ زَكَاةٌ. قَالَ: وَهُوَ قَوْلُ سُفْيَانَ

english.narrator: Sharīk → Sālim → Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr
english.text: Sharīk narrated to us, from Sālim, from Sa‘īd ibn Jubayr, who said: “There is Zakat on gold and silver jewelry.” He said: And that is the opinion of Sufyān.

arabic: حَدَّثَنَا وَكِيعٌ، عَنْ جَرِيرِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ، عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شُعَيْبٍ، عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ عُمَرَ، أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَأْمُرُ نِسَاءَهُ أَنْ يُزَكِّينَ حُلِيَّهُنَّ

english.narrator: Wakī‘ → Jarīr ibn Ḥāzim → ‘Amr ibn Shu‘ayb → ‘Abd Allāh ibn ‘Umar
